commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=40c638a0249756ac2e42eb9d0f0cf83432d75afb)
commands.review_summary:
The PR flips session metadata and transcript runtime storage from legacy JSON/JSONL files to per-agent SQLite, adding migration/doctor flows, schema changes, SDK/runtime API cleanup, docs, and broad regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: current-main and PR-head source show public helper removals and read-only doctor paths reaching SQLite open/register code.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected security/maintainer labels, draft/conflicting state, plugin API compatibility, and persisted storage migration semantics require human maintainer/security review rather than cleanup close or automated repair-only handling.

target.title: fix(agents): keep lightweight cron prompts out of no-op compaction
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96658.

Lightweight isolated cron runs on very small context windows can reserve too much of the model window before the first prompt is sent. In the reported 4,096-token fallback-model case, current `main` leaves only 2,048 prompt tokens for a 3,544-token lightweight cron prompt, so the precheck routes to `compact_only` even though the lightweight bootstrap has no useful history to compact.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps the shared small-context prompt floor conservative: `MIN_PROMPT_BUDGET_RATIO` remains `0.5` for full-context callers, settings reserve capping, CLI compaction, mid-turn guard paths, prompt-image runs, tool-enabled runs, and lightweight shared-history runs.

The higher 87.5% prompt share is scoped only to history-free lightweight pre-prompt admission with no prompt images and no active tool schemas. This intentionally does not solve provider-visible tool-schema budgeting; tool-enabled lightweight runs stay on the shared floor instead of trying to fit near the model boundary.

No SDK shape, config, docs, workflow, provider transport, or global budget policy is changed.

## User Impact

History-free lightweight cron jobs on small-context fallback models can proceed when the rendered prompt itself fits the model window, instead of entering no-op compact-only recovery with nothing useful to compact.

Tool-enabled runs, prompt-image runs, shared-history lightweight runs, and non-lightweight/full-context paths keep conservative behavior. Provider/tool-heavy budgeting is left out of scope for this PR.

## Evidence

Current-head behavior proof for the reported 4,096-token / 20,000-reserve boundary:

```text
history-free lightweight, no prompt images, no active tools: route=fits, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=3584, effectiveReserveTokens=512
history-free lightweight with prompt image: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=2048
history-free lightweight with active tools: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=2048
lightweight with shared history: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=2048
full-context mode: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=2048
```

Focused validation on head `ac34cf36327`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts
# PASS: 1 file, 21 tests

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# PASS

node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts
# PASS

git diff --check
# PASS
```

Current PR surface after narrowing:

```text
src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts                  +3/-0
src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts    +20/-1
src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts +77/-0
Total: +100/-1 across 3 files
```

Review comments addressed:

- Scope reduction: reverted the provider-visible OpenAI Responses/Completions tool-budgeting layer and its follow-up lint commit.
- Codex / ClawSweeper image follow-up: prompt-image lightweight runs stay on the shared floor.
- ClawSweeper tool follow-up, narrowed form: active-tool lightweight runs stay on the shared floor; this PR no longer claims provider/tool-heavy fits.

Autoreview: attempted one local draft-PR review round earlier, but the helper failed before review due local Codex config parsing (`service_tier = priority`; helper expected `fast` or `flex`). No autoreview findings were produced.

Agent transcript: a matching local session was found, but no transcript is included because adding sanitized transcript logs requires explicit approval.
target.author: monkeyleet

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: wangwllu

target.title: refactor: flip sessions and transcripts to sqlite storage
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Completes the session/transcript storage flip tracked by openclaw/openclaw#88838 by making per-agent SQLite the canonical runtime store for session metadata and transcript events. Legacy `sessions.json` and active transcript JSONL files are now doctor import inputs or archive artifacts, not runtime fallback state.

## Why This Change Was Made

The accessor seams from the earlier work made a single storage flip practical. Shipping a mixed file/SQLite runtime would let session metadata and transcript rows drift, so this PR moves import, runtime access, retention, SDK cleanup, and ratchets together behind the permanent accessor boundary.

## User Impact

Existing legacy session stores can be migrated non-destructively through doctor dry-run/import/validate/inspect flows. Runtime session creation, resume, transcript append, compaction, and maintenance use SQLite rows; retention still exports archived JSONL artifacts before deleting rows so support and archive workflows keep inspectable artifacts.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/check-session-accessor-boundary.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/targets.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite.test.ts test/scripts/check-session-accessor-boundary.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.conformance.test.ts src/config/sessions/store.session-lifecycle-mutation.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/session-entry-projection.contract.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/targets.ts src/config/sessions/targets.test.ts src/commands/doctor-session-sqlite.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean after two accepted findings were fixed.
- Copied local OpenClaw state proof under `/tmp/openclaw-sqlite-flip-final-20260625T013042Z`: doctor dry-run/import/validate/inspect on `codex-proof` and `codex-image-proof` reported zero issues; import wrote 2 entries plus 15 and 18 transcript events respectively.
- Isolated runtime smoke created an agent SQLite DB, created/resumed a session, appended transcript rows, compacted 4 rows to 3, and archive-before-delete cleanup removed 1 lifecycle entry after writing 1 archive artifact.
target.author: jalehman
